page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, providing 48 virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is provided via a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity features a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps, and the instance is optimized for both network and Elastic Block Store (EBS) performance.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in specific workloads. The instance achieves a strong rating in static web serving throughput at 42.15 GB/sec and in the Passmark CPU encryption test with a score of 43605.77. Geekbench image processing also registers a strong score of 15409.0. Other metrics fall within the average range, including a Passmark CPU score of 48727.93, a Redis SET operations rate of 44.3 million operations per second, and a Passmark database operations score of 21107.91. Memory bandwidth tests show 2488.72 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 164.84 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Large-model LLM inference speeds reach 8.85 tokens per second for prompt processing and 2.81 tokens per second for text generation on a 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6idn.12xlarge offers high resource density by combining a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with fast local NVMe storage and high-speed networking. This configuration reduces the need for external storage overhead in data-intensive applications. The instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, static web serving, and applications requiring high-speed local scratch space or high network throughput.
description: The Amazon Web Services r6idn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Operating at 3.5 GHz, it delivers 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) and 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, alongside a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD. The instance features a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmarks, it demonstrates strong performance in static web serving throughput at 42.15 GB/sec, Passmark CPU encryption, and Geekbench image processing, while maintaining average scores in database operations and Redis SET performance. This hardware profile provides high resource density, making it suitable for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive workloads requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, a 2850 GB NVMe SSD, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong static web serving and encryption performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized server combining high-speed local NVMe storage, dense DDR4 memory, and high-bandwidth networking for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs and 3.5 GHz clock speed
384.0 GB DDR4 memory providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed local data access
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network traffic
Strong benchmark performance in static web serving and encryption
Suited for memory-intensive databases and high-throughput web applications
